voice_note: Minimalist starter prose to bootstrap the narrative scaffolding. AI drafts, replace with your own voice when ready. Toggle off site-wide via the header `prose` button, the URL ?prose=off, or by flipping `enabled` to false here.

eras.i: The rules of the game, written before any of us showed up. Empire by other names; a central bank; a New Deal that, briefly, held the bust in place; and a postwar order built around a dollar pegged to gold.
eras.ii: After the war, an intelligence apparatus designed for a global contest. Where elected governments became inconvenient, it removed them. Iran. Guatemala. Indonesia. Chile. By 1975 the public read enough of its own files to ask what else.
eras.iii: The dollar comes off gold. Reagan rewires the social compact. September 2001 reopens the playbook for foreign wars and the bailouts that follow flow the opposite direction of the New Deal's.
eras.iv: Citizens United legalizes the money. Snowden documents the mass collection. The Epstein files surface and disappear. Palantir, OpenAI, and the data-center race come for the rest.
eras.v: Eras I through IV are about the systems. Era V is about the people running them — named, on the record, with documents and witnesses. The questions move from 'who built this' to 'is the person in the room reliable.'

anchors.y1947: The National Security Act creates the CIA. Fifteen years later, the Joint Chiefs would formally propose remote-controlling civilian aircraft and 'orchestrating terrorism in U.S. cities' as a pretext for invading Cuba. Kennedy rejected it. The document is declassified and below.
anchors.y1973: September 11, 1973. The democratically elected Allende government is overthrown in a coup whose covert U.S. financial and operational support was later documented by the Senate Church Committee.
anchors.y1975: Congress, briefly, reads its own files out loud. On the hearing-room table: the CIA's silent dart pistol — modified to fire a frozen shellfish-toxin pellet — alongside the testimony on Chile, on Cuba, on the Phoenix Program.
anchors.y2001-attack: An event still being argued about. Below: the verifiable threads from on and around the day itself. Silverstein's late lease. Zim's late move. The Dancing Israelis. The gold under WTC 4. The records in WTC 7. The passport in the rubble. Hanjour's piloting reports. The Odigo IMs. Jennings's lobby. None proves anything on its own. They are the questions, with citations.
anchors.y2001-aftermath: What followed, slowly. A CIA officer goes on television in 2007 to confirm the agency tortured. He is prosecuted in 2013. The Senate finally publishes its own study in 2014 — 6,700 pages, of which 525 are made public.
anchors.y2021-surfside: 1:22 a.m., June 24, 2021. The Champlain Towers South in Surfside, Florida partially collapses. 98 dead. NIST traces the cause to the pool deck, the corroded reinforcing steel, and warnings that had been on paper since 2018. Around the same hours, separately and on the other side of the Atlantic, John McAfee dies in a Spanish prison cell. The two threads are real and documented. What ties them together, if anything, is what the rest of the page records.
anchors.y2024-pagers: September 17–18, 2024. Thousands of pagers and walkie-talkies in use by Hezbollah operatives explode at once across Lebanon and Syria. Israel acknowledges responsibility weeks later. A supply-chain assassination at scale — the new precedent invoked any time a different device is later claimed to have done something it didn't.
anchors.y2017-bennington: Chris Cornell, May. Chester Bennington, July. Avicii, the next April. Anthony Bourdain, that June. Four high-profile deaths in fourteen months, each individually ruled suicide. A claimed documentary tying them together — 'The Silent Children' — does not appear in any verifiable production record. What is documented is each death itself, and the FBI's own 2007 bulletin on the symbols pedophiles use to identify themselves.
anchors.y2021-anthropic: December 2020. A group of safety researchers — including Dario Amodei (then VP of Research at OpenAI) and his sister Daniela (then VP of Safety & Policy) — leave to start Anthropic. They incorporate as a public-benefit corporation. The stated reason: a different vision for how AI should be scaled, and how the people building it should be trusted.
anchors.y2023-altman-firing: November 17, 2023. The OpenAI board fires Sam Altman, citing a lack of consistent candor in his communications. Five days later he is reinstated. Three of the four board members who voted to remove him are gone within weeks. The board's reasoning — and what was redacted from the public account of it — is the through-line that Ronan Farrow picks up two years later.
anchors.y2026-altman: April 2026. Ronan Farrow and Andrew Marantz publish 16,000 words in the New Yorker, the product of eighteen months of reporting, dozens of named-and-on-record sources, internal memos, and a sitting senior Microsoft executive who compares the man to Madoff and SBF. The article is the receipt.
anchors.y2026-ai-bubble: U.S. AI capital expenditure is running at roughly 5% of GDP. The historical bar at which infrastructure buildouts have busted the economy is 3%. Railroads lasted a century. Highways, fifty years. Fiber, thirty. The GPUs in this round of capex obsolesce every three to four. Daniel Priestley, who called the 2008 crash early, told Steven Bartlett what he's now saying about 2029.
anchors.y2025-kirk: Charlie Kirk is shot once in the neck at a university debate in Utah, in front of 3,000 people. The accused shooter is identified and charged within 48 hours. The official forensic record is unambiguous. The unofficial narratives are not.
